summary: Implement the M6 desktop engine version manager with strict per-project routing, checksum-visible official installation, and missing-pin recovery.

externalInputs.purpose: Confirm with an HTTP HEAD request that the exact owner-specified official artifact remains directly available without a redirect and is within the manager's 512 MiB bound; no source code was consulted.

decisions.decision: Store one strict versioned manifest at scadmill.project.json and automatically attach its exact engineVersion to every native render and export request. Resolve app-managed engines/<version>/openscad.exe before the ordinary default path and reject the executable if its reported version differs.
decisions.rationale: A single project-owned pin makes AC-15.j apply uniformly to interactive renders, ordinary exports, batch exports, and slicer handoff without parallel engine-selection paths or silent fallback to a different version.

decisions.topic: Official Windows engine installation trust boundary
decisions.decision: Expose only the exact official 2026.06.12 Windows artifact already recorded in ENGINE_VERSION. Begin download only after an explicit UI action; show the archive SHA-256 beforehand; refuse redirects; stream under a 512 MiB cap; verify archive and executable SHA-256 plus the executable-reported version; then move the complete extracted runtime into the app-data managed-engine namespace.
decisions.rationale: The engine is a separately distributed unmodified program. A narrow allow-list and complete verification provide useful version management without turning ScadMill into a general downloader or accepting a plausible but different binary.

decisions.decision: Declare futures-util 0.3.32 directly in the desktop crate to consume reqwest's existing byte stream with an enforced incremental limit. The package was already present transitively and listed in THIRD-PARTY-NOTICES under its permissive MIT OR Apache-2.0 license.
decisions.rationale: A post-download length check can allocate an unbounded response when Content-Length is absent. Incremental streaming enforces the limit while adding no new package version to the resolved dependency graph.

testEvidence.criterion: AC-15.j requires a project pinned to X to render with X even when Y is the default, and all export paths must preserve the same selection.
testEvidence.test: Create strict project manifests, exercise the real runtime with a version spy, inspect the native Tauri payload, and assert ordinary export requests inherit the project pin.

red.outcome: RED: the project-pin module did not exist and native render/version payloads carried no required engine version.

green.outcome: GREEN: strict manifest parsing, command persistence, X-over-Y runtime routing, export propagation, and native payload assertions passed.

testEvidence.criterion: FR-15.11 requires installed inventory, a checksum-visible user-initiated official download, status visibility, and a fix-it state when the project pin is unavailable.
testEvidence.test: Drive the settings and Tauri adapters with strict fixtures, assert that installation is absent before the click, then assert the exact release id, refreshed executable checksum, and mismatch fix action.

red.outcome: RED: the banner module and official list/install port were absent, and Settings displayed neither the official archive checksum nor a download action.

green.outcome: GREEN: 3 files and 8 tests passed, including no download before click, exact release selection, refreshed inventory, checksum display, and missing/present pin states.

testEvidence.criterion: The integrated capability must compile on both sides of the Tauri boundary and remain within source-policy limits.
testEvidence.test: Run the affected TypeScript slice, full static typecheck, native engine-manager tests, Rust formatting, and repository lint/source policy after one targeted correction per reported failure.

red.command: pnpm.cmd run typecheck; pnpm.cmd run lint; cargo fmt --all -- --check
red.outcome: RED: two widened test literals, one stale role query, Rust formatting, one hook dependency, and the 400-line SettingsDialog cap were reported in bounded checks.

green.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run <8 affected suites> --reporter=dot; pnpm.cmd run typecheck; pnpm.cmd run lint; cargo test --lib engine_version_manager -- --nocapture
green.outcome: GREEN: 8 TypeScript files and 73 tests passed; TypeScript passed; lint checked 595 files and source policy passed; both native engine-manager tests passed.

testEvidence.criterion: A downloaded managed engine must become usable for its pinned project even when the machine has no configured default engine.
testEvidence.test: Start the complete App with no default engine, open a project pinned to X, and require a fresh version(X) probe, enabled render controls, the pinned status label, and an ordinary auto-render job.

red.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/app/App.test.tsx -t "reprobes a project pin" --reporter=dot
red.outcome: RED: App probed only version(undefined), retained editor-only mode, and never made the newly available managed project engine usable.

green.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/app/App.test.tsx tests/application/engine/project-engine-pin.test.ts tests/ui/engine/EnginePinMismatchBanner.test.tsx tests/ui/settings/EngineVersionSettings.test.tsx --reporter=dot; pnpm.cmd run typecheck; pnpm.cmd run lint
green.outcome: GREEN: all 29 affected application, pin, banner, and settings tests passed; TypeScript, Biome, the 400-line UI cap, and platform-boundary policy passed.

attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
